title: Nassella nardoides
intro: Nassella nardoides, also known as Indian grass, is a perennial grass species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. Traditionally, Indian grass has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and sore throats, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Nassella nardoides include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Indian grass is commonly prepared as a tea or infused in oil, and it is also used in traditional remedies such as salves, poultices, and infusions to treat skin issues and wounds.
